boxlang.lsp.*.title: IDE Workspace Settings
boxlang.lsp.*.description: Configured in the IDE (VS Code: settings.json). These control LSP behavior and performance.

settings.description: When true, triggers a workspace-wide parse and index of all BoxLang files on startup and when this setting changes. Improves symbol discovery at the cost of startup time.
When true, lint diagnostics for open documents are calculated in parallel threads. Disable if you experience threading issues.
When true, enables the experimental BoxLang formatter in the IDE when not overridden by shared repo configuration.

.bxlint.json.title: Lint Configuration
.bxlint.json.description: Place .bxlint.json at the workspace root to control static analysis. Changes are detected and applied live without reloading the editor.

settings.description: Map of rule ID to rule settings. Keys are rule IDs (see Lint Rules section). Each value is an object with optional 'enabled' (boolean) and 'severity' (string) fields.
Workspace-relative glob patterns. When non-empty, only matching files are analyzed. Supports * (segment), ** (recursive), ? (single char). Always use forward slashes.
Workspace-relative glob patterns. Files matching any exclude pattern are never analyzed, even if they match an include pattern. Evaluated after include.
Map of virtual path prefix (e.g. "/models") to absolute or relative filesystem path used by the LSP during analysis and indexing. Paths are resolved relative to the workspace root.
Formatting configuration shared across IDEs for the workspace. The experimental formatter toggle lives under formatting.experimental.enabled.

rules.description: Flags local variables that are declared but never used in the code.
Flags variables that are used without an explicit scope prefix (e.g. variables.foo instead of foo).
Flags multiple method definitions with the same name within the same class.
Flags multiple property definitions with the same name within the same class.
Flags catch blocks that contain no executable code, which silently swallows exceptions.
Flags extends references to classes or interfaces that cannot be resolved.
Flags implements references to interfaces that cannot be resolved.
Flags functions with a non-void return type that lack a return statement in all code paths.
Flags local variables that share the same name as a function parameter, shadowing it.
Flags code appearing after control-flow statements like return, throw, or break that can never be executed.
Flags import statements for classes or packages that are never referenced in the file.
Flags private methods that are never called within the class, indicating dead code.

boxlang.json.title: Project Mappings
boxlang.json.description: Place boxlang.json at the workspace root (or any ancestor directory) to define virtual paths, classpaths, and module directories. Supports // line comments.

settings.description: Map of virtual path prefix (e.g. "/models") to absolute or relative filesystem path. Supports ${user-dir}, ${boxlang-home}, and ${env.VAR:default} variable expansion.
List of directories to include in the classpath for type resolution. Paths may be absolute or relative to the boxlang.json file.
List of directories containing BoxLang modules. Defaults to boxlang_modules/ relative to boxlang.json. Paths may be absolute or relative.
